title: phrygilanthus raoulii
intro: Phrygilanthus raoulii, also known as Raoul's Frangipani or Raoul's Frangipanier, is a flowering plant species native to French Polynesia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Phrygilanthus raoulii include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote healthy skin, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Phrygilanthus raoulii include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
